question: What major-specific requirements do I need to complete to earn my CS degree?
judge_reason: The generated answer fails to mention two explicitly required elements from the criteria: lab science sequences and ENGR 101, which are both listed as required components in the reference answer.
answer:
You must complete 77–78 total credits meeting these major-specific requirements:
• GPA: 2.500 for lower-division major courses, 2.000 for all major courses
• Grades: C- or higher in all major courses (except default P/NP)
• Upper-division minimum: 21 credits in the major
• Lower-division core: 23–24 credits
• Grand Challenges Initiative: 3 credits (GCI 150, 200, 250)
• Colloquium: 6 credits (six 1-credit sections of CPSC 298)
• Upper-division requirements: 25 credits
• Electives: 12 credits (at least 3 must be upper-division)
• Required GE: FFC 100B

question: What should I keep track of each semester to make sure I graduate on time?
judge_reason: The generated answer fails to mention 'degree audit' as required by the evaluation criteria, even though it provides more detailed specific metrics and requirements.
answer:
Track these key metrics each semester to ensure on-time graduation:
• Overall GPA of 2.500 for lower-division major courses
• Overall GPA of 2.000 for all required major courses
• Letter grade of C- or higher for all major courses (except P/NP)
• Minimum 21 upper-division credits in the major
• Completion of FFC 100B and the 3-credit Grand Challenges Initiative
• Fulfillment of lower-division core requirements (23-24 or 29-30 credits depending on your major)

For detailed semester-by-semester planning, contact fseadvising@chapman.edu.

question: What are the required core courses for the ISP minor?
judge_reason: The generated answer incorrectly includes CPSC 453 as a core course and omits HUM 312, which are both present in the reference answer's core course list.
answer:
The 2025-2026 catalog requires the following technical core courses (22 credits):
• CPSC 230 - Computer Science I
• CENG 231 - Systems Programming
• CENG 231L - Lab - Systems Programming
• CPSC 353 - Data Communications and Computer Networks
• ISP 363 - Cybersecurity 1
• ENGR 385 - Effective Technical Communication
• ISP 452 - Fundamentals of Digital Forensics
• CPSC 453 - Network Implementation and Security

The minor also requires one 3-credit social science course (POSC 317, POSC 341, or POSC 343) and three 1-credit sections of ISP 371 - Advanced Topics in Cybersecurity. Prior catalogs (2022-2024) used a 25-credit required core structure that included HUM 312 and POSC 317 instead of ENGR 385. All minor courses must be taken for a letter grade.

question: How does adding the Analytics minor affect my DS 4-year plan?
judge_reason: The generated answer fails to mention the critical fact that MGSC 310 and CPSC 392 are already required courses in the DS major, which is the key reason why the minor adds minimal burden and can fit into existing Year 3-4 elective slots.
answer:
Adding the Analytics minor adds 6 credits to your total degree load due to double-counting limits. The minor requires 24 credits total, but only 12 credits can overlap with your Data Science major.

- Overlapping courses (18 credits): CPSC 230, MATH 203, MGSC 220, MGSC 310, CPSC 392, MGSC 410
- Required outside courses: You must take 2 additional courses (6 credits) outside the DS major to satisfy the minor's requirement that 12 credits be taken outside the major program
- Scheduling impact: The 4-year plan is flexible for minors, but you must stay within the 18-credit semester maximum. You will need to adjust DS electives, utilize summer sessions, or extend your timeline to accommodate the extra 6 credits
- Contact fseadvising@chapman.edu to map the minor into your specific schedule

The ISP minor adds 22 credits to your SE plan. CPSC 230 and ENGR 385 overlap with your major and count toward both.

Additional courses:

- CPSC 353 (3 credits)
- ISP 363 (3 credits)
- ISP 452 (3 credits)
- CPSC 453 (3 credits)
